training.heroIntro: The City Support Center aims to meet the needs of Arab municipalities through training programs and technical consultations that strengthen institutional capacities and facilitate knowledge exchange among cities.

trainingPrograms.title: Training Programs
trainingPrograms.intro: The City Support Center aims to meet the needs and requirements of municipalities for developing technical capacities by raising the efficiency of municipal employees, building problem-solving skills, and keeping pace with technological developments in the field. It provides a suite of services including training programs aligned with global changes to improve the quality of work.
trainingPrograms.formatsTitle: These training programs are delivered through:
formats: Specialized workshops tailored to Arab municipalities, enriched with practical examples from municipal work

consulting.title: Technical Consultations & Knowledge Transfer
consulting.intro: The City Support Center is committed to providing technical support to municipalities in managing and implementing municipal projects, improving the quality of procedures and services, and learning from best practices in municipalities.

sections.title: Engineering and administrative consultations
sections.description: These consultations enhance Arab cities' capacities in various urban fields and their ability to prepare and implement studies and projects related to urban development. Technical consultations include preparing technical documents and reference specifications for urban projects, and representing municipalities by reviewing work and reports of consulting entities.

sections.title: City experience sharing
sections.description: Meetings aimed at highlighting best initiatives and practices to address urban challenges faced by member cities, forming an interactive development community that allows municipalities to discover shared urban interests and needs.

sections.title: Individual knowledge exchange
sections.description: Meetings aimed at highlighting best initiatives and practices to address urban challenges faced by member cities, forming an interactive development community that allows municipalities to discover shared urban interests and needs.

executive.title: Executive Program
executive.intro: An executive program specialized in urban development resulting from cooperation between the Center and international universities. Executive programs are designed to enhance municipal staff with global skills and knowledge, familiarity with the latest theories and practices in municipal work, and opportunities to learn how to employ technology in urban problem-solving.
programs: International Program in Innovation and Urban Management

urbanPolicies.heroIntro: The Urban Development Index in Arab cities consists of a series of surveys conducted every six months, with unified analytical reports published from the results. The index aims to monitor urban development trends and the evolution of Arab cities over time.

tabs.developmentIndex: Urban Development Index in Arab Cities
tabs.innovationLab: Urban Innovation Lab for Arab Cities

developmentPortal.title: Arab Urban Development Portal
paragraphs: The Arab Urban Development Portal is a platform for collecting and sharing data and information on cities, projects, organizations, and publications related to urban development in the Arab region.
The portal aims to strengthen knowledge exchange among Arab cities and facilitate access to reliable information sources that support urban policy and practice.
developmentPortal.contributeDescription: You can help enrich the portal by sharing information about cities, organizations, or publications related to urban development. All submissions are reviewed according to the Institute's methodology before publication.
developmentPortal.ctaDisclaimer: The inclusion of any project or organization in the portal does not necessarily imply endorsement by the Institute.

contributionForm.titlePlaceholder: Title of the city, organization, or publication
contributionForm.detailsPlaceholder: Describe the information you would like to contribute...
contributionForm.error: An error occurred while submitting. Please try again.

directory.title: Directory of Arab Cities
directory.subtitle: The Institute seeks to collect and document data, statistics, and information related to Arab cities and conduct urban research, providing a unified reference for researchers and decision-makers in urban development.

publications.name: Smart Cities Study in the Arab World

developmentIndex.title: Urban Development Index in Arab Cities
developmentIndex.intro: A specialized scientific tool for monitoring urban development pathways and trends, comparing them among Arab cities, and tracking the stability and sustainability of economic growth, demographic change, and social and cultural data at the urban level.

innovationLab.title: Urban Innovation Lab for Arab Cities
innovationLab.intro: An experimental space for developing innovative solutions to urban challenges by connecting Arab cities with global expertise and encouraging the adoption of modern technologies and practices in urban planning and management.

projects.title: Urban Innovation Lab: From Idea to Implementation
Smart Solutions for Waste Management in Arab Cities
Platform Connecting Arab Cities with Global Expertise

practiceReports.title: Urban Practices and Policies Reports
practiceReports.intro: A series of applied reports and studies documenting best urban practices and policies in Arab cities, with practical recommendations to support decision-makers in municipalities.

projects.title: Participatory Greening of Neighborhoods in Arab Cities

partnerships.heroIntro: Amid the development shifts facing Arab cities, the Arab Urban Development Institute seeks to strengthen its role as a leading think tank supporting cities through sharing advanced ideas and practices, building institutional capacities in municipalities, and serving as a platform for effective communication and knowledge exchange among cities, universities, and regional and international organizations.

euroArabDialogue.title: Euro-Arab Cities Dialogue
euroArabDialogue.intro: An expanded dialogue platform bringing together decision-makers and city leaders from Arab and European cities to exchange expertise and practices in urban development, and discuss shared challenges and cooperation opportunities in urban planning, sustainability, and innovation.

secretarySpeaks.title: The Secretary-General Speaks
secretarySpeaks.intro: A series of meetings and dialogues with the Secretary-General of the Organization of Arab Cities and municipal leaders, highlighting the visions and experiences of Arab cities in addressing urban challenges and strengthening partnerships for sustainable development.

urbanAwards.title: Arab Cities Urban Development Awards
urbanAwards.intro: A recognition initiative encouraging Arab cities to adopt best practices in urban development, and highlighting outstanding initiatives in planning, municipal management, and environmental sustainability.

partnersGuide.title: Urban Development Partners Guide
partnersGuide.intro: A comprehensive reference featuring the Institute's partner network of government institutions, universities, and international and regional organizations, providing information on cooperation areas and partnership mechanisms to support urban development in Arab cities.
